zh-tw.title: 狗狗語錄
zh-tw.description: 從毛孩的角度看人類，從狗主人的角度看自己——精選最有畫面的狗狗語錄、柴犬厭世金句與養狗才懂的日常哲學

en.title: Dog Quotes
en.description: From the dog's-eye view of human life to the truths only dog owners know — heartwarming, hilarious, and very much earned

items.sourceNote: 毛孩看到主人回家的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是出門倒個垃圾，三分鐘後回來。

但對牠來說，那是整整一個世紀。

所以牠才會用全身的力氣搖尾巴，
好像在說：你終於回來了，地球差點就要停止運轉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有狗的朋友——他下班開門那一刻，就會想起這段話
variations: 更新版：就算你只是去廁所兩分鐘出來，牠還是會用同樣熱情的方式迎接你。在牠的時間單位裡，沒有「短暫」這件事。

en.content:
You stepped outside to take out the trash. You came back three minutes later.

But for your dog, that was an entire century.

That's why its whole body wags when it sees you again,
as if to say: you finally came home, the planet almost stopped spinning.
en.editorNote: Send to any dog owner — they'll picture the exact moment they walk through their door
variations: Bonus: even if you just went to the bathroom for two minutes, you get the same homecoming. In dog time, the word 'brief' does not exist.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗散步耍賴行為，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
「來，我們回家了。」

狗狗：（直接四腳朝地，平躺路中央）

你拉牽繩，牠多加 30 公斤的隱形重量。
你蹲下來哄，牠閉眼裝睡。
你假裝要走，牠睜一隻眼確認你會不會真的拋棄牠。

這不是散步，這是談判。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養柴犬、米克斯、或任何「個性很有意見」的狗主人——他立刻就有畫面
variations: 進階版：你假裝走遠了，牠終於起身——但走兩步又躺下，告訴你：我願意走，但要照我的節奏。

en.content:
"Okay, time to go home."

Dog: (instantly flops onto the sidewalk, all four legs out)

You tug the leash — it adds 30 invisible kilograms.
You kneel down to sweet-talk it — it closes its eyes and pretends to nap.
You fake walking away — it opens one eye to confirm whether you'll actually abandon it.

This is not a walk. This is a negotiation.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner of a Shiba, mixed-breed, or any dog with strong opinions — they have lived this exact scene
variations: Advanced version: you walk far enough that the dog finally gets up — only to take two steps and lie down again, just to clarify that the walk continues on its terms, not yours.

items.sourceNote: 毛主人對狗的對話模式，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你會開始用三歲小孩的語氣，
對著一個聽不懂中文的生物說：

「寶貝你最棒了～」
「這是誰呀～是不是壞壞～」
「不可以唷～這個不可以吃～」

然後在外面，你又是一個正常的大人。
但只有你自己知道，那個三歲小孩的語氣，
才是你真正的本體。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給任何一個「在狗面前完全變成另一個人」的朋友——他會邊笑邊承認
variations: 附帶觀察：這個語氣，會無縫切換到對家人的小孩、對伴侶、最後對自己。狗狗，是你內在那個小孩的解封鑰匙。

en.content:
After you get a dog, you start using a baby voice
to talk to a creature that doesn't understand any human language:

"Who's a good boy~ you are~"
"Is that your toy? Is that your toy?!"
"No no no, we don't eat that, we don't eat that."

And then in public, you're a normal adult again.
But you and only you know — the baby voice is the real you.
The adult is just the cover story.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who turns into a completely different person around their dog — they'll laugh and admit it
variations: Bonus observation: that voice will eventually leak out toward babies, partners, and finally yourself. The dog is the key that unlocks your inner soft creature.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬厭世表情經典場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
柴犬的表情有三種：

第一種：你誰？
第二種：又是你？
第三種：你還沒走？

第四種其實也有，但只在你拿出零食的瞬間出現。
那個瞬間，牠會告訴你：好啦，我們是朋友。
（然後吃完繼續第二種。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有柴犬的朋友——他每天都在被自己的狗用眼神社交懲罰
variations: 進階版：當你想擁抱柴犬，牠會微微側身、用一隻後腿輕輕推開你。這不是拒絕，這是一份明確劃定的距離說明書。

en.content:
A Shiba Inu has three default facial expressions:

One: who are you?
Two: oh, you again?
Three: you're still here?

There's a fourth, but it only appears the second you open a bag of treats.
In that moment, it briefly admits: fine, we're friends.
(Then it finishes chewing and resets to expression number two.)
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner — they get socially humbled by their own dog daily
variations: Advanced version: when you try to hug a Shiba, it tilts its body just slightly and pushes you away with one back leg. This is not rejection — it is a clearly written boundary statement.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗乞食眼神觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是想安靜吃個便當。

但你身邊那雙眼睛說：

「我從出生到現在從來沒有吃過任何東西。」
「你手上那塊雞肉，會改變我的一生。」
「如果你不分我，我會記得，我會永遠記得。」

你最後當然分了。
因為那不是眼神，那是國家級的情緒勒索。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天在餐桌邊被狗狗用眼神勒索的朋友——他知道自己已經輸太多次了
variations: 附帶警告：你只要分過一次，那個眼神會升級成「定期繳費系統」，每餐都會準時出現在你旁邊。

en.content:
You just wanted to eat a quiet meal.

But the eyes next to you are saying:

"I have never eaten anything in my entire life."
"That piece of chicken in your hand will change me as a person."
"If you don't share, I will remember. I will remember forever."

You shared, of course.
Because those aren't eyes. That's a fully credentialed emotional negotiator.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who loses every staring contest at dinner — they already know they've surrendered too many times
variations: Warning: share food once, and those eyes upgrade into a recurring subscription. They will appear next to your plate at every single meal from then on.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗忠誠陪伴主題，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠不知道你今天工作多累。
牠不知道你被誰誤會、被誰冷落。
牠不知道你為什麼坐在沙發上不說話。

牠只知道：你回來了，你在這裡，那一切就還可以。

所以牠會走過來，把頭靠在你腳邊。
那不是寵物，那是這個世界送你的小型穩定器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得有點累、家裡有狗的朋友——他會看完這段，然後低頭看一下他的狗
variations: 延伸版：你流眼淚的時候，牠不會問你怎麼了，牠只會更靠近一點。原來最高級的安慰，是不需要語言的。

en.content:
Your dog doesn't know how exhausting your day was.
It doesn't know who misunderstood you or who let you down.
It doesn't know why you're sitting on the couch saying nothing.

It only knows: you're home, you're here, so things are okay again.

That's why it walks over and rests its head against your foot.
That's not a pet. That's the small stabilizer the world sent you.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been a little tired lately and has a dog at home — they'll finish reading and look down at theirs
variations: Extended version: when you cry, the dog doesn't ask what happened. It just moves closer. Turns out the highest form of comfort doesn't need language.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗洗澡前的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你還沒拿出洗澡盆。
你還沒打開水龍頭。
你只是「想到」要幫狗狗洗澡。

牠就已經消失了。

剛剛還在客廳搖尾巴的生物，現在縮在沙發底下，眼神帶著「你怎麼可以」的譴責。

狗狗沒有讀心術，牠只是把你研究了五年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次洗澡都要把狗從沙發底下拖出來的朋友——他會邊苦笑邊點頭
variations: 進階版：你假裝沒事繼續滑手機，等牠放鬆走出來，再突然抓起牠衝向浴室。這個策略，每次都失敗，但每次你都還是會試。

en.content:
You haven't taken out the bathtub yet.
You haven't turned on the faucet yet.
You just thought about giving the dog a bath.

It's already gone.

The creature that was wagging its tail in the living room is now jammed under the sofa, looking at you with full "how dare you" energy.

Dogs aren't telepathic. They've just studied you for five years.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to extract a dog from under the couch on bath day — they'll wince and nod
variations: Advanced version: you pretend nothing's happening and scroll your phone. The moment the dog relaxes and walks out, you grab it and sprint to the bathroom. This plan fails every time. You will try it again next week.

items.sourceNote: 狗主人手機相簿觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，你的手機相簿會出現一個很奇怪的現象：

7000 張狗狗的照片。
400 張狗狗的影片。
3 張你自己的照片，而且其中兩張，狗狗也在裡面。

你已經不是這個帳號的主角了。
你是一個攝影師，全職拍一個不會看你成品的客戶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次打開相簿都覺得「我自己呢？」的毛主人——他會立刻去檢查自己的相簿
variations: 附帶觀察：你發 IG 限動，狗狗的那則永遠比你出去玩的那則收到更多愛心。你接受了這個事實，因為你也覺得狗狗比較可愛。

en.content:
After you get a dog, a strange thing happens to your camera roll:

7,000 photos of the dog.
400 videos of the dog.
3 photos of yourself, two of which also feature the dog.

You are no longer the main character of this account.
You're a full-time photographer for a client who will never review your work.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner whose camera roll has been quietly taken over — they'll check it immediately and laugh
variations: Bonus: your dog posts on Instagram always get more likes than your vacation photos. You accepted this peacefully, because deep down, you also think the dog is cuter.

items.sourceNote: 雨天狗狗散步抗議，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
下雨天，你穿好雨衣、套好牠的小雨衣，準備帶牠出門。

牠走到門口，看了一下外面。
牠抬頭看你。
牠回頭看一下沙發。
牠又看了一下你，眼神告訴你：「我們今天可以不去嗎？」

你考慮了 2 秒。
你脫下雨衣。
你跟牠一起回沙發。

誰是主人，這時候已經很清楚了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次雨天都會被狗狗說服改成「在家陪坐」的主人——這就是真實生活
variations: 進階版：晴天的時候，牠會在門口暴衝。牠的態度，是天氣決定的，不是你決定的。

en.content:
It's raining. You put on your raincoat, fasten the dog's tiny matching one, and head for the door.

It walks to the entrance. Looks outside. Looks up at you.
Looks back at the couch.
Looks at you again, with eyes that say: "Could we just… not, today?"

You think about it for 2 seconds.
You take off the raincoat.
You walk back to the couch with the dog.

Who the owner is here — that's been settled for a while now.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has been quietly persuaded by their dog to stay in on rainy days — this is everyday life now
variations: Advanced version: on sunny days, the same dog turbo-launches at the door. Its attitude is determined by the weather, not by you.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗門口迎接的工作觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家狗狗的職業是：全職門口接待員。

工作內容：
上班時間 24 小時。
沒有休息日。
薪水是兩餐飼料、一條牽繩、無限量的擁抱。

牠每天最重要的 KPI，就是讓你回家的那一刻，覺得自己是世界上最被期待的人。

而牠每天都達標。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗的朋友——這是他每天回家最被需要的「公司福利」
variations: 附加條款：偶爾請假的時候，牠會用沙發鞋子作為一份不滿意度評語，提醒你他的工作不該被忽視。

en.content:
Your dog has a full-time job: door greeter.

Responsibilities:
On shift 24 hours a day.
No days off.
Compensation: two meals of kibble, one leash, unlimited hugs.

Its most important daily KPI is to make sure that the moment you walk through the door, you feel like the most anticipated person in the world.

It hits the target every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to any dog owner — this is the most reliable employee benefit in their life
variations: Fine print: when the greeter takes a personal day, you'll find a chewed shoe on the couch — that's a written performance review reminding you that the job exists.

items.sourceNote: 柴犬抗拒散步躺地經典場景，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
柴犬散步的步驟：

1. 你說「走吧」，牠興奮衝出門。
2. 走 50 公尺，牠突然停下。
3. 牠決定現在這裡是世界中心。
4. 牠平躺。
5. 你拉牽繩，牠變成 30 公斤的石頭。
6. 路人經過，露出同情的微笑。
7. 你也微笑回應，假裝這一切都在計畫中。

計畫從來不在你手上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個被柴犬「定點抗議」過的主人——他知道這個畫面太真實
variations: 結局版：等牠決定起身，你已經感謝了三位路人、解釋了五次「牠就是這樣」，並且重新評估了你跟一隻狗的權力關係。

en.content:
How a Shiba Inu walk works:

1. You say "let's go," the dog explodes out the door.
2. 50 meters in, it stops cold.
3. It decides this exact spot is now the center of the universe.
4. It lies down.
5. You pull the leash. It becomes a 30-kilogram boulder.
6. A stranger walks by, smiling sympathetically.
7. You smile back, as if all of this was the plan.

The plan was never yours.
en.editorNote: Send to any Shiba owner who has been forced into a public sit-down protest — this is uncomfortably accurate
variations: Ending: by the time the dog decides to stand up, you've thanked three strangers, explained "it just does this" five times, and quietly reassessed your entire power dynamic with a small fluffy animal.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗等門等待主人回家觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你出門那一刻，牠就坐在門口。

牠不知道你去哪裡。
牠不知道你會去多久。
牠只知道：這扇門，是你會回來的地方。

所以牠就坐在那裡。
從早到晚。
從晴天到下雨。
從你還愛自己，到你已經不太喜歡自己。

牠都還在等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近覺得自己不夠好、家裡有狗的朋友——這段話會讓他想起，原來有人從來沒有放棄相信他
variations: 附加版：你終於開門那一刻，牠不會問你去哪、為什麼這麼晚——牠只會用全部的力氣告訴你：你回來了，這就夠了。

en.content:
The moment you leave, your dog sits at the door.

It doesn't know where you went.
It doesn't know how long you'll be gone.
It only knows: this door is where you come back.

So it sits there.
From morning to night.
From sunshine to rain.
From the version of you who still liked yourself, to the version that's been struggling lately.

It's still waiting.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been hard on themselves lately and has a dog at home — they'll remember that someone never stopped believing in them
variations: When you finally open the door, it doesn't ask where you went or why you're late. It just tells you, with everything it has: you're home. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到其他狗的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你家狗狗在路上看到另一隻狗。

選項一：原地暴衝、瘋狂搖尾巴，好像對方是失散三十年的親人。
選項二：完全裝沒看到，傲嬌地把頭轉開。
選項三：對方明明很小，牠卻嚇到躲到你身後，並且表示「我從來沒看過這種怪物」。

沒有人知道牠會選哪一個。
包括牠自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗、每次散步都在猜「今天會是哪個版本」的朋友——他每天都在驚喜
variations: 附帶觀察：對方主人會根據你家狗狗的反應，決定是要走過來打招呼，還是抱著自己的狗繞道走。這是一場無聲的社交談判。

en.content:
Your dog spots another dog on the street.

Option A: charges forward, tail spinning, as if reunited with a long-lost relative.
Option B: pretends to see nothing, turns its head away with full aristocratic energy.
Option C: the other dog is tiny — but yours hides behind your leg and signals "I have never seen such a creature in my life."

No one knows which one will play.
Including the dog.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who plays the daily lottery of "which version of my dog will show up today" — every walk is a surprise
variations: Bonus observation: the other owner adjusts their plan based on your dog's reaction. They either walk over to say hi, or scoop up their dog and reroute. It's a silent social negotiation, and your dog is leading it.

items.sourceNote: 毛孩陪伴度過低潮主題，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
人會離開。工作會結束。朋友會走散。
但你回家時，牠永遠會在門口。

而且牠不會問：你最近還好嗎？
牠會直接告訴你：你還好，因為我在這裡。

養狗最神奇的事情，不是牠陪了你多少天，
而是那些你以為自己撐不過去的日子，
你都撐過去了——而牠就睡在你旁邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近過得很辛苦、家裡有毛孩的朋友——這段話會落在他需要被接住的位置
variations: 延伸版：你以為是你在養牠。其實很多時候，是牠在養你。一個眼神、一次靠近，就讓你願意再多撐一天。

en.content:
People leave. Jobs end. Friendships drift apart.
But when you come home, your dog is always at the door.

And it doesn't ask: how have you been?
It just tells you: you're okay, because I'm here.

The magical thing about having a dog isn't how many days it's been with you.
It's that all those days you thought you couldn't get through —
you got through.
And it was sleeping next to you the whole time.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's been having a tough stretch and has a dog at home — this will land exactly where they need it to
variations: Extended: you thought you were the one taking care of the dog. Most of the time, the dog has been quietly taking care of you. One look, one nudge closer — and you decide to make it through one more day.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗睡覺佔床觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你買了一張很貴的雙人床。

第一天晚上，你睡正中央。
第二天晚上，狗狗來了，你睡左邊三分之二。
第三天晚上，你睡邊邊一條，棉被被牠捲走一半。
第四天晚上，你掛在床沿，慶幸自己還沒掉下去。

那張床的所有權，從第二天就已經不是你的了。
你只是繳貸款的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天晚上跟狗狗搶床、最後總是輸掉的朋友——他會邊苦笑邊認領這個身份
variations: 進階版：你想翻身，狗狗會發出一聲低沉的嘆氣，提醒你：你的動作打擾到了真正的房客。

en.content:
You bought an expensive queen-size bed.

Night one: you sleep in the middle.
Night two: the dog joins, you sleep on the left third.
Night three: you're on a strip near the edge, half the blanket has been claimed.
Night four: you're hanging off the side, grateful you haven't fallen.

The ownership of that bed transferred on night two.
You are just the person paying the mortgage on it.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who quietly loses the bed war every night — they'll laugh and accept the title
variations: Advanced version: you try to roll over and the dog lets out a deep sigh, reminding you that your movement just inconvenienced the real tenant of the bed.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對快遞員與門鈴的反應，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
門鈴一響，你家狗狗的反應分三段：

第一段：地震級狂吠，好像家裡來了八個入侵者。
第二段：你開門，是熟悉的快遞大哥。
第三段：牠尾巴搖得比你還開心，繞著對方腳邊轉圈，順便幫你簽收。

你問牠剛剛兇什麼。
牠看你一眼，意思是：「我只是先建立氣勢，這是禮節。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次門鈴一響就要先安撫狗狗的朋友——這個流程他每週都跑好幾遍
variations: 附加觀察：等快遞大哥走了，牠還會多吠兩聲，告訴你：「我剛剛守住了這個家，記得加菜。」

en.content:
When the doorbell rings, your dog's response comes in three acts:

Act one: earthquake-level barking, as if eight intruders just broke in.
Act two: you open the door — it's the same delivery guy as always.
Act three: tail wagging at full speed, circling his legs, basically signing for the package on your behalf.

You ask what all the barking was about.
The dog gives you a look that says: "I had to set the tone first. It's just etiquette."
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who has to defuse the dog every time the doorbell rings — this is a weekly routine for them
variations: Bonus: after the delivery person leaves, the dog barks twice more, just to inform you, "I successfully defended this home today. Treats would be appropriate."

items.sourceNote: 狗狗年紀變大、變慢的觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
牠年輕的時候，跑得比你還快。
你一拿牽繩，牠就衝到門口，眼神告訴你：快點，世界在等我們。

後來有一天，你拿起牽繩，牠慢慢走過來。
再後來，你叫牠，牠抬起頭花了兩秒才反應。

你才發現：原來陪你的這個小朋友，已經比你想像中走得更遠了。

但牠還是會在你回家時站起來，努力搖兩下尾巴。
那份歡迎，從來沒有變慢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡狗狗已經慢慢變老的朋友——這段話他會看完，然後安靜地過去摸牠一下
variations: 延伸版：你開始走得比牠慢一點，停下來等牠。原來這些年，牠也是這樣等你的，只是你那時候沒有察覺。

en.content:
When it was young, it ran faster than you.
The second you picked up the leash, it sprinted to the door, eyes saying: hurry up, the world is waiting for us.

Then one day, you pick up the leash, and it walks over slowly.
Later, you call its name, and it takes two seconds to lift its head.

That's when you realize: this small friend has actually traveled further with you than you noticed.

But it still stands up when you come home, and gives the tail two soft wags.
That welcome — it never slowed down.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose dog has been getting older quietly — they'll finish reading and walk over to pet theirs
variations: Extended: you start walking a little slower, pausing so it can catch up. Turns out it's been doing the same for you all these years — you just didn't notice back then.

items.sourceNote: 外星人視角看遛狗的觀察句改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
如果現在有外星人飛過來地球，看到一個畫面：

一個人類，手上拿著繩子。
繩子的另一端，是一隻小型生物。
那個人類，正彎下腰、用塑膠袋把小生物的便便撿起來。

外星人會默默把報告寫成這樣：

「地球的主導物種，已經確認。
體型較小、毛茸茸、不付房租的那一種。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天認真撿便的毛主人——他會邊苦笑邊承認這份報告寫得太準
variations: 進階版：外星人離開前會補一句：「並且那個小生物，會用一個眼神，命令較大的物種改變散步路線。權力結構非常清楚。」

en.content:
If aliens flew to Earth right now, here's the first scene they'd witness:

A human, holding a rope.
On the other end of the rope, a small creature.
The human is bending over, using a plastic bag to pick up the small creature's poop.

The aliens would file this report:

"Dominant species confirmed.
It's the smaller, fluffier one. The one not paying rent."
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who dutifully picks up after their dog — they'll wince at how accurate the report is
variations: Bonus line in the report: 'And the small creature can change the larger one's walking route with a single look. The hierarchy here is unambiguous.'

items.sourceNote: 狗主人家規逐步退讓的觀察改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗第一天的家規：

第一條：狗狗不能上沙發。

養狗第三十天的家規：

第一條：狗狗可以上沙發，但不能上床。

養狗第一百天的家規：

第一條：狗狗可以上床，但要睡腳邊。

養狗第三百天的家規：

第一條：人類睡覺前要先確認狗狗在哪一邊，再決定自己睡哪裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給養狗一年以上、家規早就重寫過好幾次的朋友——他會點頭笑說「我家現在已經到第四條了」
variations: 終極版：第三百六十五天，家規只剩下一條——狗狗開心就好。然後人類安靜地搬了一個小床墊到地上。

en.content:
House rules, day one:

Rule one: the dog stays off the couch.

House rules, day thirty:

Rule one: the dog can be on the couch, but not on the bed.

House rules, day one hundred:

Rule one: the dog can be on the bed, but only at the foot.

House rules, day three hundred:

Rule one: before going to sleep, the human checks where the dog is, and then decides which side they get.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner past the one-year mark whose house rules have been quietly rewritten several times — they'll laugh and admit they're already on version four
variations: Final form: by day 365, only one rule survives — the dog is happy. Then the human quietly moves a small mattress onto the floor.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗吃醋情緒觀察改寫，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在路上摸了別的狗。

你以為這是一件小事。
你以為牠不會發現。
你以為你回家洗手就沒事。

但牠在門口聞到你身上的味道，整隻狗的氣場立刻變了。
牠不看你。牠走到角落，背對著你坐下。
你叫牠，牠耳朵動一下，但頭不轉。

那不是賭氣，那是一份正式的書面抗議。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些「忍不住摸到別的狗、回家被自己家狗冷處理」的朋友——這個情境他這個月已經發生三次
variations: 進階版：你拿出零食試圖和解，牠會先吃，但吃完繼續背對著你。這代表：賄賂收下了，但這件事還沒過。

en.content:
You petted another dog on the street.

You thought it was no big deal.
You thought your dog wouldn't notice.
You thought washing your hands at home would cover it.

But at the door, your dog catches the scent on you, and the entire energy of the room shifts.
It doesn't look at you. It walks to a corner and sits with its back to you.
You call its name. One ear twitches. The head doesn't turn.

That isn't sulking. That's a formal written complaint.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who can't resist petting other dogs and gets the cold shoulder at home — this has happened to them three times this month
variations: Advanced: you offer a treat as a peace offering. The dog eats it. Then turns its back again. The bribe is accepted; the matter is not closed.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗在家工作干擾觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
在家工作 + 養狗 = 一場永遠不會結束的會議干擾。

你開鏡頭，牠走過來舔你的下巴。
你關鏡頭，牠去叼一個會發出尖銳叫聲的玩具。
你拜託牠安靜，牠用更大的力氣咬第二下。
你假裝沒聽到，牠跳上你的椅子。
你終於投降，把牠抱起來——

下一秒，會議的所有同事都看到了一隻狗的下巴。

那一刻，那隻狗成為這場會議的主角。
你只是會議室裡那個拿著鍵盤的助理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天 WFH 都被狗狗插嘴開會的朋友——他的同事早就習慣了那張毛茸茸的臉
variations: 附帶觀察：你的同事們已經先問狗狗好，再問你工作進度。從某個時間點開始，那隻狗的存在感比你更強。

en.content:
Work from home + a dog = a meeting that will be interrupted forever.

Camera on: it walks over and licks your chin.
Camera off: it picks up a squeaky toy.
You beg for quiet: it bites the toy harder, on purpose.
You pretend not to hear: it jumps onto your chair.
You finally surrender and pick it up —

the next second, the entire meeting is staring at a dog's chin.

In that moment, the dog becomes the main character of the call.
You're just the assistant in the room with the keyboard.
en.editorNote: Send to any WFH friend whose dog crashes every meeting — their coworkers have memorized that fluffy face by now
variations: Bonus: your coworkers now greet the dog first, and ask about the project second. At some point, the dog officially out-presenced you on your own calls.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗看到行李箱反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是把行李箱從衣櫃裡拿出來。
你還沒打開，你還沒裝任何東西。

但牠已經坐在行李箱旁邊，眼神告訴你三件事：

第一：你不可以走。
第二：如果你硬要走，請把我裝進去。
第三：如果裝不下，那這個行李箱我先躺下。

你最後當然把行程改短了一天。
那不是內疚，那是被一隻狗管理的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次出門前都要被狗狗用眼神挽留的朋友——他知道行李箱根本不是他的
variations: 進階版：你回家那天，行李箱還沒打開，牠已經繞著它走三圈，確認你這次有沒有把牠忘在某個城市。

en.content:
You just pulled the suitcase out of the closet.
You haven't opened it. You haven't packed anything.

But your dog is already sitting next to it, eyes communicating three things:

One: you may not leave.
Two: if you must leave, pack me in.
Three: if I don't fit, then I am claiming this suitcase as a bed.

You ended up shortening the trip by a day.
That's not guilt. That's a life being managed by a small dog.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone who gets emotionally negotiated with every time they pull out a suitcase — the suitcase was never theirs
variations: Advanced version: when you come home, the dog circles the unopened suitcase three times — checking whether you accidentally left it in some other city.

items.sourceNote: 狗狗聽到「散步」關鍵字反應觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
養狗之後，家裡會出現一份「不能說的詞彙清單」：

「散步」要改說「外面活動」。
「洗澡」要改說「水的事情」。
「醫院」要直接拼音、用代號、最好換語言。

但牠永遠比你聰明一步。
你才剛改完代號三天，牠就學會了新代號的意思。

你發現自己每天都在跟一隻狗鬥智，而且常常輸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都在家裡跟狗狗用代號講話的朋友——他家的暗號已經換到第十版
variations: 附帶觀察：你跟另一半在客廳用英文討論「等一下要不要帶牠出去」，牠抬頭看你，眼神說：「我懂英文，下次請說法文。」

en.content:
After you get a dog, your house develops a list of forbidden words:

"Walk" gets replaced with "outside time."
"Bath" becomes "the water thing."
"Vet" gets spelled out, code-named, and ideally translated into another language.

But the dog is always one step ahead.
Three days after you swap in a new code word, it has already learned what it means.

You realize you're in a daily battle of wits with a small animal — and you're losing more often than not.
en.editorNote: Send to any owner who speaks in code at home — they're already on version ten of the secret vocabulary
variations: Bonus: you and your partner discuss "taking it out later" in English in the living room. The dog looks up with eyes that say, 'I understand English. Try French next time.'

items.sourceNote: 狗狗對主人手機嫉妒觀察，原創語錄

zh-tw.content:
你坐在沙發上滑手機。

第一次：牠走過來，把下巴放在你手機上。
第二次：牠用鼻子把你的手機頂開。
第三次：牠直接坐在你手機跟你的眼睛中間，正面對你。

那個眼神告訴你：「那塊發光的東西不會愛你，我會。」

你最後當然放下手機。
你已經被一隻毛茸茸的生物上完一堂關於陪伴的課。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次滑手機都被狗狗插隊的朋友——他會邊笑邊承認，自己已經被教育很多次了
variations: 進階版：牠發現用爪子撥手機沒用之後，會升級成直接躺到你的鍵盤上，告訴你：「今天的工作到此為止。」

en.content:
You're on the couch scrolling your phone.

First attempt: the dog walks over and rests its chin on your phone.
Second attempt: it noses the phone aside.
Third attempt: it sits directly between your phone and your eyes, facing you.

The look says: "That glowing thing will never love you. I will."

You put the phone down, of course.
A fluffy creature has just delivered a fully formed lecture on presence.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone whose dog keeps interrupting their scrolling — they'll laugh and admit they've taken this class many times
variations: Advanced version: after pawing at the phone fails, the dog upgrades to lying directly on your keyboard, gently announcing: 'Work is over for today.'
